scope: Actual LightningIndexer layer-2 compressed dKV backward on 1xMI350 at S4096, CR=4, topk=512, H=64, D=512.
why_this_artifact_exists: The dense-group HIP probe reduced modeled writes but regressed to 121.8 ms at S4096. This records why that is not a full-layer regression and pins the next implementation rung so future work does not loop back into scalar local accumulation.

not_a_production_regression_read.bad_probe_scope: One isolated scalar dense local compressed-group x D16 HIP reducer. It consumes diagnostic materialized probs and grad_logits and times only the compressed dKV accumulation/write path.
not_a_production_regression_read.read: The 121.8 ms number is not the current full-layer fwd+bwd latency and should not be used to update the full-layer gap. It rejects one local-accumulation schedule: scalar LDS/local atomic work can reduce global write count while destroying MFMA/GEMM tile efficiency.

localized_wall.read: Dot math is small, direct sparse atomics land on the 8-9 ms global accumulation wall, and partial-store proves the wall is not just final cross-tile atomic contention. The failed dense-group HIP branch shows that local write coalescing must be MFMA-shaped; scalar per-selected/group/D loops are the wrong body.

positive_floors_to_preserve.read: MFMA-friendly KxS by SxD accumulation is capable of beating the atomic path, and route metadata is sub-ms. The production kernel must keep those two positives while avoiding the multi-GiB score/prob or dense-route slabs used by the floors.

operator_formula.compressed_dkv: For each compressed group g and D16 block, dKV[g, d] += sum_{token t, head h, selected slot k where group(t,k)=g} grad_logits[t,h,k] * scale * q[t,h,d] + probs[t,h,k] * dO[t,h,d].
operator_formula.desired_tile_compute: Inside one query tile, form selected-key contributions with MFMA-shaped dot-K or equivalent Kx(rows) by rowsxD16 contractions, segment them by compressed group, reduce locally, then write one reduced D16 row per touched group instead of per-token/per-selected global atomics.

implementation_contract.backend_surface: gfx950 HIP/CK-Tile/rocPRIM PyTorch extension for the accumulator body; Triton remains acceptable for route flags/packing and small probes.
implementation_contract.do_not_edit: Do not edit third_party checkouts. Keep the implementation under the experiment tools or TorchTitan WIP changesets.
implementation_contract.input_contract_first_stage: Diagnostic stage may consume materialized probs, grad_logits, safe_groups, valid_u8, and active-group lists to isolate dKV accumulation. Production stage must fuse score/probability recompute, LI derivative, and compressor gradients without saving selected K/V/prob slabs.
implementation_contract.layout_contract: Preserve query-major selected-key reuse. Do not switch the whole backward to group-major row recompute, do not materialize dense [H,S,G] route weights, and do not materialize [B,H,S,K] scores/probs at S4096.
implementation_contract.global_write_contract: Write compressed dKV once per touched compressed-group and D16 tile, or fuse downstream LI/compressor gradients so this intermediate write is avoided entirely.

first_executable_rung.name: 32x256_D16_materialized_probs_smoke
first_executable_rung.why_first: This halves selected span relative to the primary target while preserving nontrivial write reduction and the same scratch envelope. It is the compile/parity/perf smoke, not the final target.

primary_target_rung.name: 32x512_D16_actual_li_layer2
primary_target_rung.why_primary: It keeps the full coalesced-write target while staying under the 128 KiB p99 scratch budget. It is the smallest retained rung that can plausibly move the 8-9 ms dKV wall toward the 4.7-7.1 ms MFMA floors.

dynamic_unique_group.requirement: Do compaction inside the fused kernel or with a sub-ms metadata kernel. External torch.unique/scatter/BMM loops are rejected.

fixed_panel.requirement: Only retry fixed panels as a fused in-kernel compaction/accumulation body. PyTorch panel scatter+BMM loops and dense-panel scalar loops are already rejected.

correctness: S128 parity against reference dKV, then S512 parity. Use the existing BF16 tolerance envelope already accepted for actual-LI dKV probes; add FP32/tighter smoke if the kernel exposes a clean oracle path.
Exact route metadata parity for active flags, counts, and packed group lists when using the dynamic-unique branch.
No saved selected K/V, no saved selected probability slab, and no dense [H,S,G] route tensor at S4096.
performance: The S512 isolated accumulator must beat the rejected scalar branches: active-list scalar 3.13 ms, fixed-panel scalar 3.08 ms, dense-group HIP 4.14 ms, and dot_k_sparse local 8.06 ms.
The first S4096 32x256/D16 timing must beat the direct sparse-atomic wall around 8.6-8.8 ms, otherwise write coalescing is not paying for itself.
The S4096 32x512/D16 target should move toward the 7.05 ms mixed flat-GEMM floor while avoiding its 3.4-5.6 GiB materialization footprint; a stretch target is the 4.73 ms BF16 flat-GEMM floor.
reject_immediately_if: The kernel reaches high modeled write reduction but S512 is slower than the scalar local accumulators.
The body loops scalar over selected groups/heads/D in a way that cannot map the main contraction to MFMA.
Target-shape memory climbs because scores/probs, dense route weights, or selected K/V are materialized.
S4096 timing is dominated by one HIP kernel like the 120.07 ms dense-group trace.

next_actions: Add a new HIP/CK-Tile entrypoint beside the existing scalar accumulator, not by mutating the scalar body into the production path.
Smoke 32x256/D16 on S128 and S512 with materialized probs/grad_logits to isolate the accumulator.
If S512 passes the promotion gate, run S4096 timing-only and rocprof kernel trace on one MI350 under /scratch/sonle5/dsv4_pretrain_canary_20260527.
Only after the accumulator is close, fuse target_probs, KL derivative, LI dQ/dK/dW, and compressor gradients into the query-major recompute path.
